summary: Excellent matrix recording of a hot 1978 Vermont show, highlighted by smoking Big River, Looks‑Like‑Rain and a fire‑filled Jerry Garcia throughout.
review: The tape captures a high‑energy 1978 UVM performance that fans repeatedly call a “hot little show” with standout renditions of Big River, Peggy‑O, Loser, Looks Like Rain, Playin’ in the Band and Me & My Uncle.  The mix is a sweet late‑70s soundboard paired with an intimate audience feed, delivering warm, full‑bodied sound that many describe as heavenly despite occasional keyboard burying.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is ~4.7/5; reviewers overwhelmingly praise the performance and the recording quality, with only minor complaints about keyboard presence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sweet warm late‑70s soundboard (SBD) is full and clear; audience portion adds intimate raw intensity.  Overall mix is crisp, though keyboards can be buried in the AUD track and occasional minor boominess is noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as fast and punchy, second set slows into classic Dead grooves with strong jams; pacing praised for keeping momentum while allowing deep improvisation.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is repeatedly called “on fire”, perfect tone and soaring leads throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass gets positive nods – reviewers ask Phil to turn it up, noting his solid support.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard presence is spotty; several listeners note Keith (or Brent) missing or buried in the mix, especially on the audience portion.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work shines, with “Drumz > Wharf Rat” highlighted as intense and driving.
